title: WIP: BESE tokenizer — 38-token structured alphabet + BPE, 288 vocab, 12.9MB model
author: mrbese
body:
## Summary

- **BESE (Base-Efficient Subword Encoding):** a novel two-layer tokenizer that replaces the standard 1,024-token BPE vocabulary with a 38-token linguistically structured alphabet, then applies 250 BPE merges on top (total vocab: 288)
- The 73% vocabulary reduction saves ~295KB of embedding parameters — enough for 2-3 extra transformer layers in a 16MB model
- Model size: **12.9 MB** int8+zlib (vs 13.6 MB baseline)
- **Status: WIP** — tokenizer verified correct, full-data training run pending

## Approach

**Layer 1 — Structured alphabet (38 tokens):**
- 8 most frequent English letters (e,t,a,o,i,n,s,r) → single-token encoding (1 byte each)
- 18 remaining letters → 5 context-disambiguated groups + position markers (2 tokens, 1 byte)
- Groups designed via bigram frequency analysis: letters sharing a group appear in distinct contexts
- Case-insensitive (model learns capitalization from context); preserves exact UTF-8 byte counts

**Layer 2 — BPE on structured tokens:**
- Standard BPE trained on the BESE token stream (not raw bytes)
- 250 merges compress sequences to comparable lengths as baseline
- Merges capture more meaningful patterns because base tokens are linguistically structured

**Key innovation:** Trading vocabulary size for model depth. The saved embedding parameters fund extra transformer layers, increasing the model's representational capacity within the 16MB budget.

## What we've done so far

- Built complete tokenizer with verified byte-accurate BPB accounting (100/100 docs pass)
- Fast BPE trainer (O(N log N) vs naive O(N*M))
- Full training pipeline: decode SP shards → train BPE → export BESE shards → train GPT
- Initial 1xH100 run: model trains normally (train loss 5.69→1.22), but used 80x less data due to Python bottleneck (now fixed)
- Just fixed critical BPE training bugs (node-0 merge count corruption, stale position drift)

## What's next

- Full-data retrain with all 10 shards (~1B tokens) using fixed fast tokenizer
- Architecture tuning: convert parameter savings into extra layers (target 13L vs 9L baseline)
- Hyperparameter sweep on merge count and model dimensions

## Files

- `tokenizer/` — BESE constants, base tokenizer, fast BPE trainer
- `integration/train_gpt_bese.py` — Modified training script with BESE LUT support
- `scripts/` — RunPod pipeline, shard export, BPE training from JSONL
- `docs/` — Experiment results, integration guide

## Repo

Full source: https://github.com/mrbese/parameter-golf/tree/bese-submission
